title: manihot tristis subsp. saxicola
intro: Manihot tristis subsp. saxicola, also known as the rock cabbage or mountain breadroot, is a subspecies of the Manihot tristis plant, a type of edible root vegetable native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Manihot tristis subsp. saxicola include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, aid in weight loss, and provide relief from gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as decoctions and infusions, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
